sections.title: 1. Online Reservation Reimbursement
sections.content:
Log in to the Fudan University Finance Department Integrated Information Portal at www.cwgl.fudan.edu.cn, click "Reservation Reimbursement" to enter the "Reimbursement Form Management" page, then click "Apply for Reimbursement Form" and fill in the required basic reimbursement information to generate a reservation reimbursement form.

The reservation reimbursement system contains five types of business: regular reimbursement, domestic travel reimbursement, temporary loans, remuneration declaration, and internal fund transfer through on-campus settlement points.

Regular reimbursement includes office expenses, printing expenses, consulting fees, handling fees, utilities, postal and telecommunications fees, property management fees, transportation expenses, overseas travel expenses, repair expenses, rental expenses, conference expenses, training expenses, hospitality expenses, material expenses, commissioned service fees, fixed asset purchases, book purchases, building repair expenses, retired staff activity expenses, student activity expenses, and similar items.

Domestic travel reimbursement covers inter-city transportation, accommodation, and related expenses incurred during official business travel.

Temporary loans apply to payments for which invoices cannot be obtained in advance, including advance funds for large conferences, imported equipment purchases, and similar matters.

Remuneration declaration covers staff remuneration, expert consulting fees, lecture fees, manuscript fees, review fees, rehiring fees, student work-study payments, scholarships, grants, and labor service fees for external personnel.

Internal fund transfer through on-campus settlement points applies to internal transactions or fund transfers within the university, including on-campus testing fees, processing fees, newspaper and journal subscriptions, telephone charges, meal charges, accommodation charges, conference venue fees, warehouse material collection, and similar items.

sections.title: 2. Common Online Reservation Payment Methods
sections.content:
The common payment methods for online reservation reimbursement include personal bank-card transfer, bank remittance, and other payment methods.

In accordance with the Interim Regulations of the People's Republic of China on Cash Management, the use of cash shall be strictly controlled, and payments exceeding RMB 1,000 shall be settled through the banking system.

(1) Personal bank-card transfer: This method applies to payments that comply with the cash management regulations and are below RMB 1,000. For a single invoice or consecutively numbered invoices with an amount exceeding RMB 1,000, a POS card slip, online transaction payment certificate, or similar proof shall be attached. Expenditures included in the mandatory official card settlement catalogue shall be settled with an official card.

(2) Bank remittance: Economic transactions with institutions shall be paid by bank remittance. When making the reservation, the payee institution's account name, bank, and account number shall be filled in accurately.

(3) Other payment methods: Other payment methods shall be selected for foreign exchange purchases for overseas travel, write-off of temporary loans, taxes related to advance tax invoices, check issuance, and similar matters.

sections.title: 3. Required Reimbursement Materials
sections.content:
(1) The printed "Fudan University Reservation Reimbursement Form" generated through reservation on the Finance Department portal website shall be submitted. For projects with a paper fund booklet, the accounting entry copy from the fund booklet shall also be submitted. The "Fudan University Reservation Reimbursement Form" shall bear the signatures of the project principal investigator and the handling staff member. The signing project principal investigator shall be the same person as the project principal investigator recorded in the financial system. If the project principal investigator cannot sign for special reasons, the reason shall be explained, and the project principal investigator shall authorize another signatory by signature, with the authorization filed with the Finance Department.

(2) Original supporting invoices shall bear the signatures of the project principal investigator and the handling staff member. For expenditures involving university-allocated funds, the designated "single-signature" approver shall sign. For research project funds, the research project principal investigator may sign. For special business requiring approval by relevant departments, approval signatures from those departments shall also be obtained. If an invoice is an electronic invoice, it shall first be authenticated in the reservation system, and the corresponding electronic invoice shall then be selected when filling in the reimbursement form.

(3) For business such as travel expenses, overseas travel, conference expenses, equipment purchases, and transfer of research funds, the relevant approvals, documents, reports, explanatory notes, and similar supporting materials shall be provided.

(4) For any single payment of RMB 30,000 or more, a signed payment contract shall be attached. One party to the contract shall be Fudan University, and the contract shall bear Fudan University's contract seal. For capital construction and renovation expenditures, a contract shall be attached regardless of the amount.

(5) For any single payment exceeding RMB 50,000, the "Fudan University Application Form for Large Cash Fund Payments" shall be completed, signed by the head of the project unit, and affixed with the official seal.

sections.title: 4. Negative List of Reimbursement Invoices
sections.content:
(1) No supervision seal from the finance or tax authority.
(2) The scope of use of the invoice does not match its stated content.
(3) Incomplete information, such as missing issuance date, quantity, unit price, amount, or business description.
(4) No financial seal or invoice seal of the payee institution.
(5) The amount in words and figures is inconsistent.
(6) Altered, tampered with, or false invoices.
(7) Consecutively numbered taxi invoices or a large number of invoices from the same taxi.
(8) Incomplete reimbursement procedures or similar deficiencies.

sections.title: 5. Binding, Submission, and Inquiry for Reimbursement Materials
sections.content:
(1) All reimbursement documents shall be organized by the reserved reimbursement category and fastened with a stapler or clip. If there are many supporting documents, they shall be placed in a suitable envelope and sealed to prevent scattering or loss. The reservation reimbursement form shall serve as the cover sheet and shall be firmly stapled to the reimbursement documents or the envelope.

(2) The reservation form and reimbursement materials shall be submitted to the relevant business acceptance point.
Handan Campus: regular reimbursement forms and domestic travel reservation forms shall be placed in the "General Business Box" in Room 104; student remuneration reservation forms shall be placed in the "Student Remuneration Box" in Room 104; remuneration reservation forms for faculty and external personnel shall be submitted to Room 106; internal fund transfer business through settlement points shall be submitted to the corresponding on-campus settlement point.
Fenglin Campus: general business materials shall be submitted to Room 107, Building East 1; on-campus remuneration business materials shall be submitted to Room 109.
Zhangjiang Campus: materials shall be submitted to Room 205, Administration Building.

(3) Reservation forms shall be delivered and scanned correctly according to business type. Except for payments exceeding RMB 1,000,000, the Finance Department generally completes processing within 3 working days, or within 5 working days at year-end or during winter and summer breaks. Applicants may check the reimbursement progress under "Reimbursement Form Management" and then "View Reservation Logistics." If any abnormality is found, the applicant shall promptly contact the Finance Department.

(4) If finance staff find that an expenditure does not comply with reimbursement rules, or have doubts about invoices or attachments during review, they will notify the reimbursement applicant by text message or telephone using the contact information on the reservation form. The applicant shall promptly retrieve the problematic documents from the relevant reviewer according to the text message notice, complete the required supplementary procedures, and resubmit the reimbursement.

(5) Problematic documents that do not meet the requirements shall be retrieved by the applicant within 3 months from the date of the text message notice. The Finance Department will no longer retain overdue problematic documents. The retention period may be appropriately extended during winter and summer breaks. If the applicant cannot retrieve the documents within the prescribed period for special reasons, the applicant shall contact the relevant reviewer in advance and explain the situation, and the retention period may then be appropriately extended.

sections.title: 6. Reimbursement of Office Expenses and Printing Expenses
sections.content:
(1) Routine office supplies that do not meet the fixed asset threshold, such as office stationery, computer accessories, office furniture, books, newspapers, journals, cleaning supplies, and drinking water, shall be charged under office expenses.

(2) Low-value consumables, office furniture, computer accessories, and similar items that fall within the Asset Management Office's review scope for purchased inventory shall first obtain the approved "Fudan University Purchased Inventory Review Result Confirmation Form" from the Asset Management Office. For office supplies and cleaning supplies that do not fall within that review scope, a goods list provided by the seller or an online purchase transaction detail record shall be attached.

(3) Book and reference material expenses shall generally be accompanied by a detailed list. If the price of a single book or set reaches RMB 1,000 or above, or an equivalent amount in foreign currency, it shall be registered with the Humanities and Social Sciences Library and a fixed asset addition form shall be obtained.

(4) The printing of materials, papers, examination papers, and various publicity materials, as well as information dissemination expenses such as novelty search and retrieval, paper review and publication fees, page charges, and monograph publication subsidies, shall be charged under printing expenses.

(5) For photocopying fees, printing and production fees, and similar expenses where a single invoice or invoices issued in the same period total more than RMB 1,000, a detailed list shall be provided. Invoice amounts shall not be artificially split in order to avoid the review requirement for providing a list.

(6) A monograph publication subsidy shall be supported by the original or a copy of the publishing contract. If the contract party is an individual or a department, approval and signature from the department responsible for project fund administration shall also be obtained.

sections.title: 7. Reimbursement of Travel Expenses
sections.content:
(1) When on-campus faculty members travel on official business, or when external scholars, experts, and other off-campus personnel are invited to the university for conferences, exchanges, or visits and inter-city transportation, accommodation, and similar expenses are incurred, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" shall be completed for reimbursement, signed by the head of the unit, and affixed with the official seal of the department or first-level budget unit. If the unit head travels personally, the form shall be countersigned by another relevant leader.

(2) Standards for travel class by means of transportation.
The corresponding personnel categories and transportation standards are as follows:
Academicians, senior liberal arts professors, and other scholars of equivalent status; personnel in management posts at national post salary grade 2 or above; persons at ministerial rank or equivalent; academicians of the Chinese Academy of Sciences or Chinese Academy of Engineering and specially appointed senior liberal arts professors; Changjiang Scholars; recipients of the National Science Fund for Distinguished Young Scholars; principal investigators of major research projects, limited to the relevant major research project; and professional technical personnel at national post salary grade 2 may be reimbursed, upon proof, for train soft sleeper, first-class soft seat, business class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, first-class ship cabin, and first-class or business class airfare.
Bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, including professors, research fellows, chief physicians, and first-class arts personnel; professional technical personnel at vice-senior rank or above under the position-salary system; senior engineers, senior economists, senior accountants, associate professors, associate research fellows, associate chief physicians, second-class arts personnel, and equivalent or higher technical personnel at grade 5 or above, including grade 5; and management personnel at deputy division level or above may be reimbursed, upon proof, for train soft sleeper, first-class soft seat, first-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, second-class ship cabin, and economy class airfare.
All other personnel may be reimbursed, upon proof, for train hard seat, hard sleeper, second-class soft seat, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, third-class ship cabin, and economy class airfare.

The principal investigator of a horizontal research project may choose an appropriate means of transportation within the project budget according to actual research needs. If project personnel travel in a class higher than the prescribed standard, a written explanation of special circumstances shall be provided and approved and signed by the project principal investigator.
If the relevant research project management rules or contract prescribe specific standards for transportation, those provisions shall prevail.

(3) Accommodation standards.
Travelers shall follow the accommodation standard corresponding to their rank:
Ministerial-level standard: persons at ministerial rank or equivalent, and academicians of the two academies and specially appointed senior liberal arts professors.
Bureau-director-level standard: bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, including professors, research fellows, chief physicians, and first-class arts personnel; and senior engineers, senior economists, senior accountants, associate professors, associate research fellows, associate chief physicians, second-class arts personnel, and equivalent or higher technical personnel at post salary grade 5 or above, including grade 5.
Other personnel standard: all remaining personnel.
The accommodation limits are shown in Appendix 1, Detailed Table of Fudan University Domestic Travel Accommodation Limits. These standards apply to all project funds.

(4) Reimbursement of travel expenses shall be supported by the relevant transportation and accommodation invoices. For research projects that require field investigation in rural or remote areas where transportation invoices cannot be obtained, reimbursement may be made according to actual circumstances after an approved explanation of special circumstances and other valid supporting materials are provided. If accommodation charges are incurred but no accommodation invoice can be obtained, an approved explanation of special circumstances together with a receipt personally signed by the payee may be used for reimbursement within the accommodation standard, and meal allowance and local transportation expenses may be paid according to the prescribed standards.

(5) When staff members attend conferences or training sessions away from campus, the conference or training notice shall be provided for reimbursement. If the organizer arranges meals and accommodation in a unified manner, no meal allowance or transportation subsidy shall be paid during the conference or training period. Conference service fees and document fees that are paid shall be reimbursed together with the travel expenses.

(6) Faculty and staff may claim meal allowance and local transportation allowance on the basis of round-trip inter-city transportation invoices and accommodation invoices. If both inter-city transportation and accommodation during the trip are borne by another institution, no meal allowance or local transportation expense shall be paid. If one of those expenses is borne by another institution, an explanation of special circumstances approved and signed by the project principal investigator shall be provided. With supporting materials such as a conference notice, training notice, invitation letter, or other valid certificate issued by the other party, meal allowance and local transportation expense may still be paid.

(7) If travelers conducting fieldwork or investigation must drive themselves or rent a vehicle due to geographical environment, local conditions, or other special reasons, they shall provide an explanation of special circumstances approved and signed by the project principal investigator, and vehicle rental fees, fuel expenses, and bridge and toll road fees may be reimbursed within the budget limit upon proof. In principle, the total of those expenses shall be controlled within the lowest standard for inter-city public transportation.

(8) Meal allowance shall be calculated by natural calendar days of travel and paid as a lump-sum amount according to regional standards: RMB 120 per person per day in Xinjiang, Qinghai, and Tibet, and RMB 100 per person per day in other regions.

(9) Local transportation expenses may be reimbursed against invoices within the budget limit, or used as a lump-sum amount of RMB 80 per person per natural calendar day of travel.

(10) If transportation expenses or meal allowance have already been borne by an external institution, they shall not be claimed again from the university. If the trip is made by self-driving or rental vehicle, no local transportation allowance shall be paid.

(11) The transportation and accommodation standards for invited external personnel shall be determined by the project principal investigator by reference to on-campus personnel of the same level, but no meal allowance or lump-sum local transportation allowance shall be paid.

sections.title: 8. Reimbursement of Overseas Travel Expenses
sections.content:
(1) Reimbursement for overseas travel shall be supported by the original or a copy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" issued by the Overseas Affairs Center of the Foreign Affairs Office, together with the original overseas expense statement. The overseas expense statement includes conference registration fees, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, inter-city transportation, and similar items.

(2) Air tickets related to the overseas assignment, together with boarding passes, as well as accommodation, conference registration fees, inter-city transportation, visa fees, insurance fees, and similar expenses, shall be reimbursed only after review and signature by the Overseas Travel Service Center of the Foreign Affairs Office.

(3) For official overseas travel, the transportation class standard for research project funds and similar funds shall follow Article 7 on travel expense reimbursement. For non-research project funds and similar funds, the standards are as follows:
Ministerial-level personnel: first-class airfare, deluxe soft sleeper or business class seat on all-soft-seat trains, and first-class ship cabin.
Bureau-director-level personnel: business-class airfare, train soft sleeper or first-class seat on all-soft-seat trains, and second-class ship cabin.
Other personnel: economy-class airfare, train hard seat or second-class seat on all-soft-seat trains, and third-class ship cabin.

(4) In principle, international airfare, accommodation, and similar expenses incurred during short-term overseas travel shall be settled by official card or other bank transfer methods. Overseas expenses that have been paid but for which travel has not yet taken place shall not be reimbursed. Travelers shall complete reimbursement procedures promptly after returning to China. Only when the overseas stay exceeds one month or other special circumstances exist may temporary borrowing procedures for airfare and accommodation be handled in advance.

(5) All overseas travel expenses shall be reimbursed in a single consolidated batch. Expenses for books, materials, and similar items purchased abroad for work purposes during the overseas trip shall be reimbursed together with the overseas expenses. If goods are purchased from a foreign website and no formal receipt can be obtained, in addition to self-printed proof of purchase, a written commitment regarding authenticity and uniqueness shall also be attached.

(6) When reserving reimbursement for payments made in foreign currency, the corresponding foreign currency shall be selected. The reservation reimbursement system will convert the foreign-currency amount into RMB according to the foreign exchange rate published by the People's Bank of China. If multiple currencies cannot be reserved at the same time, the amount may also be converted into RMB by the applicant according to the exchange rate on the payment date, and proof of the exchange rate on that payment date shall be attached.

sections.title: 9. Reimbursement of Conference Expenses
sections.content:
(1) Conference expenses are subject to comprehensive quota control, and the amounts for different expense items may be adjusted among themselves. The comprehensive quota covers accommodation, meals, conference room rental, transportation, document printing, office stationery, medical expenses, and similar items. For conferences where accommodation or meals are not arranged, the applicable comprehensive quota shall be the standard after deducting the accommodation or meal component. Invited experts' travel expenses, rental of simultaneous interpretation equipment, interpreter service fees, consulting fees for participating experts, lecture fees, and labor service fees are not included in the comprehensive quota and may be reimbursed within the budget based on actual costs.

1. If conference expenses are reimbursed from research project funds, the Interim Measures of Fudan University for the Administration of Conference Expenses under Research Project Funds and Similar Funds shall apply.
The comprehensive quota standards for conference expenses under research project funds and similar funds are as follows, in RMB per person per day:
Domestic business conferences: accommodation 500, meals 150, other expenses 100, total 750.
Domestic management conferences: accommodation 340, meals 130, other expenses 80, total 550.
International conferences held in China: accommodation 700, meals 200, other expenses 300, total 1,200.
Other expenses include conference room rental, transportation, document printing, office stationery, medical expenses, and similar items.

2. If conference expenses are reimbursed from non-research project funds and similar funds, domestic conferences shall follow the relevant expenditure standards in the Measures for the Administration of Conference Expenses of Central and State Organs (Cai Xing [2016] No. 214), and international conferences shall follow the relevant expenditure standards in the Measures for the Administration of International Conferences Held in China (Cai Xing [2015] No. 371), as shown in Appendix 2.
The comprehensive quota standards for conference expenses of central and state organs are as follows, in RMB per person per day:
Category I conferences: accommodation 500, meals 150, other expenses 110, total 760.
Category II conferences: accommodation 400, meals 150, other expenses 100, total 650.
Category III and IV conferences: accommodation 340, meals 130, other expenses 80, total 550.
Other expenses include conference room rental, transportation, document printing, office stationery, medical expenses, and similar items.

(2) Reimbursement of conference expenses shall be supported by the conference notice, including the agenda, the conference expense budget form, the detailed conference expense list, the sign-in sheet of actual participants, including participants' names and institutions, and materials provided by the conference service provider such as invoices, original detailed supporting documents, electronic settlement slips, and entrusted agreements or contracts.

(3) Conference expenses shall be reimbursed in a single consolidated batch. If reimbursement in installments is genuinely necessary because different settlement channels are involved, reimbursement may be made separately by channel, but only once for each channel. For reimbursement made in installments, the detailed conference expense list shall include the details of expenses reimbursed previously.

(4) For regular work meetings or seminars, if it is necessary to arrange a working meal, the list of participants and the detailed conference expense list shall be provided, and actual reimbursement may be made within the conference expense budget at a standard not exceeding RMB 50 per person.

(5) When hosting or organizing large conferences, advance payment of conference expenses is allowed. In principle, the advance amount shall be controlled within 20% to 30% of the conference budget. The conference notice, including the agenda, the conference budget form, and relevant conference contracts shall be attached when requesting the advance. For international conferences, the relevant approval documents shall also be attached.

(6) A domestic conference attended by foreign guests shall be approved by the Foreign Affairs Office.

(7) The time for conference registration and departure shall be strictly controlled. Domestic business conferences and international conferences shall not exceed 2 days, and domestic management conferences shall not exceed 1 day.

(8) In principle, conferences attended mainly by institutions based in Shanghai, meaning more than 50% of participants are from Shanghai-based institutions, shall not be held outside Shanghai. Conferences shall not be held in scenic areas prohibited by explicit directives of the CPC Central Committee or the State Council.

sections.title: 10. Reimbursement of Training Expenses
sections.content:
(1) Training expenses include instructor fees, accommodation, meals, training venue fees, training material fees, transportation, and other expenses. Except for instructor fees, training expenses are subject to classified comprehensive quota standards, itemized verification, and total amount control, and the amounts for different items may be adjusted among themselves. The comprehensive quota standards are as follows, in RMB per person per day:
Personnel at ministerial level and equivalent: accommodation 500, meals 150, venue/materials/transportation 80, other expenses 30, total 760.
Bureau-director-level personnel: accommodation 400, meals 150, venue/materials/transportation 70, other expenses 30, total 650.
Division-level and lower personnel: accommodation 340, meals 130, venue/materials/transportation 50, other expenses 30, total 550.

(2) Training of up to 30 days shall be controlled according to the comprehensive quota standard. For training exceeding 30 days, the excess days shall be controlled at 70% of the comprehensive quota standard. The number of days above includes arrival and departure time, and arrival and departure shall each not exceed 1 day.

(3) Reimbursement of training expenses shall be supported by the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" approved by the relevant school or department, the list of trainees and their contact information, and proof such as the detailed expense statement provided by the training service provider.

(4) Instructor fees refer to expenses incurred for hiring instructors to teach, including lecture fees, accommodation, meals, and inter-city transportation for the instructors. Instructor fees are calculated separately and are not included in the comprehensive quota. The standards for lecture fees of instructors shall follow the labor service fee standards for lecture fees after tax. Inter-city transportation for instructors shall follow the relevant travel expense rules and standards, while accommodation and meals shall follow the standards in the comprehensive quota for training expenses.

sections.title: 11. Reimbursement of Family Visit Travel Expenses
sections.content:
(1) Unmarried faculty and staff visiting their parents, and married faculty and staff visiting their spouse, may be reimbursed for one round-trip fare per year in accordance with the rules.

(2) For married faculty and staff visiting their parents, the portion of the round-trip fare that exceeds 30% of the employee's monthly basic salary may be reimbursed. Such reimbursement may be made once every four years.

(3) Family visit travel expenses refer to inter-city transportation expenses, including long-distance bus, train, and ship fares. Local transportation expenses such as taxi fares and bus fares shall not be reimbursed. If air travel is used, the reimbursable amount shall be calculated by reference to the train fare standard.

(4) Faculty and staff who meet the eligibility requirements for family visits shall complete the "Fudan University Faculty and Staff Family Visit Application and Approval Form" and submit it for reimbursement after approval by their department and approval and seal by the Human Resources Office.

sections.title: 12. Declaration and Inquiry of Remuneration
sections.content:
(1) Remuneration and labor service fees paid to internal or external personnel shall in principle be transferred through the banking system, and cash payments shall not be made.

(2) When making a reservation declaration, the major business category shall be selected as "Remuneration Declaration," which is divided into three categories: internal personnel, students, and external personnel. The declarant shall accurately fill in information such as the personnel category and the nature of the remuneration.

(3) For the first declaration of labor service fees for an external person, relevant information such as the person's ID card number or passport number, bank card number, and bank routing number shall first be entered under "External Personnel Information Maintenance." Finance staff will review the information within 3 working days. Only after the review is approved may the handling staff member fill in the labor service fee information under "Remuneration Declaration." If the external person is a foreign national who cannot obtain a domestic bank card, the labor service fee may be transferred to the handling staff member's bank card for onward payment, provided that the "Fudan University Detailed Form for Payment of Labor Remuneration to Foreign Personnel" personally signed by the foreign recipient is obtained.

If payment fails or is returned by the bank because of incorrect information for external labor personnel or for other reasons, the handling staff member shall promptly relay the relevant notice from the Finance Department to the person concerned and, after obtaining the correct information, register it with the Finance Department. Finance staff will reissue the payment based on the registered information.

(4) If remuneration is to be credited to the bank card within the same month, the relevant materials shall be submitted to the Finance Department before the 23rd day of that month.

(5) After remuneration has been paid to internal personnel, they may check salary, remuneration, housing subsidies, and similar payments, as well as individual income tax payments, through the "New Finance Inquiry" system. Due to bank posting processes, the actual arrival time may lag slightly behind the time of remuneration entry.

sections.title: 13. Standards for Labor Service Fees
sections.content:
(1) Fees for graduate thesis review and defense.
Doctoral thesis: 5 to 8 reviewers, at RMB 300 to 500 per person; 5 to 7 defense committee members, at RMB 300 to 500 per person; 1 defense secretary, at RMB 150.
Master's thesis: 2 to 3 reviewers, at RMB 200 to 300 per person; 3 to 5 defense committee members, at RMB 200 to 300 per person; 1 defense secretary, at RMB 100.

(2) Lecture fees, after tax.
For professionals with associate senior technical titles, the lecture fee shall not exceed RMB 500 per class hour. For professionals with full senior technical titles, it shall not exceed RMB 1,000 per class hour. For academicians and nationally renowned experts, it shall generally not exceed RMB 1,500 per class hour.
Lecture fees shall be calculated based on actual class hours delivered, with a maximum of 4 class hours for each half day.
Lecture fees for other personnel shall be determined by reference to the above standards.
If the same lecture is delivered simultaneously to multiple classes, the lecture fee shall not be calculated repeatedly.

(3) Expert consulting fees.
For persons with senior professional technical titles or equivalent:
On-site consultation: RMB 500 to 800 per person per day for the first and second days, and RMB 300 to 400 per person per day from the third day onward.
Written or remote consultation: RMB 60 to 100 per person per project.
For other personnel:
On-site consultation: RMB 300 to 500 per person per day for the first and second days, and RMB 200 to 300 per person per day from the third day onward.
Written or remote consultation: RMB 40 to 80 per person per project.

sections.title: 14. Approval and Payment for Engineering and Renovation Expenditures
sections.content:
(1) Engineering and renovation expenditures shall be supported by the original "Fudan University Approval Form for Engineering Project Payments and Large Cash Fund Payments" approved by the university's Capital Construction Office. The approval form shall bear the official seal of "Fudan University Capital Construction Office" and the "Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office." For engineering projects exceeding RMB 100,000, the approval form shall also bear the approval signatures of the director of the Capital Construction Office and the vice president in charge.

(2) Payments for engineering and renovation items shall be made strictly according to the payment amount and payment schedule agreed in the contract. Overpayment is not allowed, and payment shall not be made in advance ahead of the agreed progress.

(3) When paying the first installment for an engineering project, the "Fudan University Renovation Project Construction Contract" shall be provided, and payment shall be requested according to the proportion and amount agreed in the contract.

(4) At the time of final settlement after project completion, the originals of the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" and the "Fudan University Construction Project Final Settlement Audit Filing Form" shall be provided. The "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" shall be reviewed by the cost review company and affixed with its official seal, and shall also bear the "Capital Construction Office Review Process" seal from the Capital Construction Office. In principle, the final payment price of the project shall be based on the "approved total settlement amount" stated in the opinion and shall not exceed the contract price. The "Fudan University Construction Project Final Settlement Audit Filing Form" shall bear the signature or seal of the project management department, as well as the receipt acknowledgement and handling opinion of the Audit Office.

sections.title: 15. Reimbursement of Warranty Retention and Cost Review Fees
sections.content:
(1) The invoice amount for engineering, equipment, and similar expenditures shall be issued for the full amount, including a certain proportion retained as warranty retention. The Finance Department will issue a dedicated warranty retention receipt according to the warranty retention ratio. After the warranty period expires, and provided that there is no dispute over the quality of the engineering work or equipment during the warranty period, the department may apply to the Finance Department for the return of the warranty retention by presenting the warranty retention receipt signed by the department head.

(2) Engineering cost review fees shall be borne jointly by the university and the construction contractor. The handling staff member shall reserve reimbursement according to the amount of the cost review fee that should be included in the university's construction cost as stated in the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion." The portion of the cost review fee to be borne by the construction contractor does not fall within the scope of reimbursement, but a copy of the cost review fee invoice issued in the name of that contractor shall be provided.

sections.title: 16. Reimbursement of Fixed Assets, Intangible Assets, and Purchased Materials
sections.content:
(1) Purchased experimental instruments, equipment, furniture, books, and other tangible goods with a unit price exceeding RMB 1,000, as well as large quantities of similar materials whose unit value does not reach the prescribed standard but whose service life exceeds one year, excluding exactly one year, shall be treated as fixed assets. Reimbursement of fixed assets shall be accompanied by the "Fudan University Asset Reimbursement Form" (second copy). Fixed assets other than books shall be registered with the Asset Management Office, and books shall be registered with the library.

(2) Application software valued at RMB 2,000 or more, inclusive, that does not constitute an indispensable component of related hardware shall be treated as an intangible asset. Reimbursement shall be accompanied by the "Fudan University Asset Reimbursement Form (Intangible Assets)" (second copy) issued by the Asset Management Office.

(3) Experimental materials and other consumable supplies that do not meet the standards for fixed assets or intangible assets shall be treated as purchased materials. Reimbursement shall be accompanied by the "Fudan University Purchased Inventory Confirmation Form (Based on Review Results)."

sections.title: 17. Reimbursement of Instrument and Equipment Maintenance Fees
sections.content: Before reimbursing maintenance fees for office equipment, research equipment, and similar items, an application shall first be submitted through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" shall be printed. After it is signed by the relevant personnel and stamped by the affiliated school or unit, it shall be submitted together with the original supporting invoices for reimbursement.

sections.title: 18. Reimbursement of Official Hospitality Expenses
sections.content:
(1) Reimbursement for domestic official hospitality shall be settled on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. Reimbursement documents shall include financial invoices, an official letter from the visiting institution, the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List," and the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form." The official letter from the visiting institution shall include the content of the official activity, the itinerary, and the list of participants.

(2) Accommodation expenses shall not be charged for official activities. If accommodation must be arranged for the guests, it shall generally be arranged on campus, mainly in standard rooms. For officials at provincial-ministerial level, an ordinary suite may be arranged. In principle, accommodation shall not be arranged for official activities involving institutions from the same city.

(3) If a working meal is genuinely necessary for an official activity, it shall in principle be arranged as a boxed meal or buffet. The standard for ordinary meals shall not exceed RMB 100 per person per day. One working meal may be arranged when appropriate, and the standard shall not exceed RMB 150 per person. The number of accompanying diners shall be strictly controlled. If the number of guests is 10 or fewer, the number of accompanying diners shall not exceed 3. If the number of guests exceeds 10, the number of accompanying diners shall not exceed one third of the number of guests. When a working meal is arranged, accompanying staff may be provided with a guest meal at a standard not exceeding RMB 50 per person.

(4) Travel related to official activities shall be arranged through centralized transportation, vehicle types shall be used appropriately, and accompanying vehicles shall be strictly controlled.

sections.title: 19. Reimbursement of Visitor Reception Expenses
sections.content:
(1) Visitor reception shall be carried out strictly in accordance with the project budget, and reimbursement shall be settled on a one-matter, one-settlement basis.

(2) For meal expenses incurred in domestic visitor reception activities, the receiving unit shall truthfully complete the "Fudan University Domestic Reception List" and have it reviewed and signed by the relevant responsible person. The reception list shall include the visitor's institution, name, and reason for the visit, among other details. Meal standards and vehicle arrangements shall follow the domestic official hospitality rules. Inter-city transportation and accommodation expenses shall follow the relevant provisions of Article 7 on travel expense reimbursement.

(3) International travel expenses, accommodation, daily meal expenses, banquet expenses, transportation, gifts, and similar costs incurred when inviting foreign guests to visit the university, as well as international travel expenses incurred when they attend conferences, shall be governed by the relevant standards in the Measures for the Administration of Reception Funds for Foreign Guests of Central and State Organs. The receiving unit shall truthfully report to the Foreign Affairs Office and obtain the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report," which shall be reviewed and signed by the relevant responsible person and signed by the handling staff member.

The standards for foreign guest reception expenses shall follow the relevant expenditure standards in the Measures for the Administration of Reception Funds for Foreign Guests of Central and State Organs (Cai Xing [2013] No. 533), as shown in Appendix 3.

Accommodation, meal expenses, and similar costs incurred when inviting foreign guests to attend conferences shall follow the relevant rules on conference expenses in Article 9.

sections.title: 20. Temporary Loans and Write-off
sections.content:
(1) For ordinary payment business, invoices shall generally be obtained before applying for expense reimbursement. For special business such as the purchase of imported equipment or patent fees, where invoices cannot be obtained in advance, a temporary loan may be applied for.

(2) The borrower shall be a Fudan University employee on the payroll, a leased employee whose employee number begins with R or L, or a postdoctoral researcher. The borrower shall sign the "Fudan University Reservation Reimbursement Form" for confirmation.

(3) Retired personnel, students, and external personnel may not act as borrowers for temporary loan business.

(4) The borrower shall complete the write-off procedures in a timely manner within the prescribed period. For loans settled within the same city, the write-off shall generally be completed within 30 days. For loans settled in another location, the write-off shall generally be completed within 60 days.

(5) If the borrower has an outstanding loan that has not yet been written off, the Finance Department will not accept a new loan application from that borrower unless special reasons exist.

(6) Before leaving the university, faculty and staff shall settle all loans and outstanding charges.

(7) When writing off a temporary loan, the applicant shall accurately fill in the basic reimbursement information, select the project code of the temporary loan business to be written off, choose "Other Payment Method" and "Regular Reimbursement Business," tick the corresponding temporary loan business, and finally fill in the write-off amount.

sections.title: Appendix 2: Expenditure Standards for International Conferences Held in China (for Non-Research Project Funds and Similar Funds)
sections.content:
Conference categories:
Category I international conferences: international conferences attended by ministerial-level officials as official representatives.
Category II international conferences: international conferences attended by bureau-director-level officials as official representatives.
Category III international conferences: international conferences attended by division-level and lower officials or other personnel as official representatives.

Expenditure standards:
Venue rental, calculated by the number of official representatives: Category I RMB 300 per person per day; Category II RMB 200 per person per day; Category III RMB 150 per person per day.
Equipment rental: Category I RMB 100 per person per day; Category II RMB 100 per person per day; Category III RMB 100 per person per day.
One cold buffet reception or cocktail reception for the opening or closing ceremony, including beverages and service charges, calculated by the number of official representatives: Category I RMB 220 per person per occasion; Category II RMB 180 per person per occasion.
Number of staff: controlled within 10% of the number of official representatives, and resident staff shall not exceed 50% of all conference staff.
Staff board and lodging expenses: RMB 450 per person per day.
Where volunteers cannot eat on time because of work needs, meal provision or meal-delay subsidies: RMB 100 per person per day.
Interpreter service fees: simultaneous interpretation in a United Nations official language, RMB 5,000 per interpreter per day; simultaneous interpretation in a language other than a United Nations official language, RMB 6,000 per interpreter per day; written translation, RMB 200 per thousand Chinese characters.
Rental of simultaneous interpretation equipment and office equipment, calculated by the number of official representatives: RMB 100 per person per day.
Transportation, namely vehicle rental fees: large bus with more than 25 seats, RMB 1,500 per vehicle per day; medium bus with 25 seats or fewer, RMB 1,000 per vehicle per day; passenger car with 5 seats or fewer, RMB 800 per vehicle per day.
Other conference service expenses, calculated by the number of official representatives: RMB 100 per person per day.

sections.title: Appendix 3: Expenditure Standards for Reception of Foreign Guests by Central and State Organs
sections.content:
Expenditure items and standards by personnel category:

Accommodation venue:
Foreign guest delegations led by personnel at vice-ministerial level or above: five-star or four-star hotels.
Delegations led by bureau-director-level personnel or below, as well as other ordinary foreign guest delegations: no higher than four-star hotels.

Daily meal expenses for foreign guests, including alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages:
Heads of state and heads of government: RMB 600 per person per day.
Vice presidents, vice premiers, and speakers or deputy speakers: RMB 550 per person per day.
Ministers and vice ministers: RMB 500 per person per day.
Other personnel: RMB 300 per person per day.

Banquet expenses for foreign guests, including alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages:
Banquets hosted by ministers or vice ministers: RMB 400 per person per occasion.
Banquets hosted by bureau-director-level personnel or below: RMB 300 per person per occasion.
Cold buffet reception: RMB 150 per person per occasion.
Cocktail reception: RMB 100 per person per occasion.
Tea reception: RMB 60 per person per occasion.

Means of transportation for foreign guests visiting other localities:
Personnel at vice-ministerial level or above: first-class airfare, first-class ship cabin, and soft-seat rail travel, including business class seats on high-speed rail or EMU, first-class seats on all-soft-seat trains, and deluxe soft sleeper trains.
Other personnel: economy-class airfare, second-class ship cabin, and soft-seat rail travel, including first-class seats on high-speed rail or EMU, first-class seats on all-soft-seat trains, and soft sleeper trains.

External gifts:
If the giver or recipient is at ministerial or vice-ministerial level: no more than RMB 400 per person per occasion.
If the giver or recipient is at bureau-director level: no more than RMB 200 per person per occasion.
Other personnel: small commemorative gifts may be given as appropriate.

Accompanying personnel:
For major diplomatic or foreign affairs activities receiving foreign guests at the level of head of state or head of government, the number of Chinese participants attending banquets shall be arranged according to protocol requirements.
For other banquets, where there are 5 or fewer foreign guests, the number of Chinese and foreign attendees shall in principle be arranged within a ratio of 1:1. Where there are more than 5 foreign guests, the excess portion shall in principle be arranged within a ratio of 1:2.

Meal-delay subsidy for reception staff: RMB 50 per person per occasion.
